Description: Mark widgets as const whenever possible to prevent unnecessary rebuilds and improve performance.
Do: Use const for all stateless widgets and immutable subtrees: const Text('Hello'), const SizedBox(height: 16)
Don't: Use non-const constructors for static widgets; omit const keyword on Padding/Text/Icon/SizedBox

Description: Split complex Widget trees into named private widgets or methods to improve readability and enable const optimization.
Do: Extract subtrees into _HeaderWidget, _CardRow, etc. as StatelessWidget subclasses
Don't: Keep 200+ line build() methods; use inline Builder callbacks for all subtrees
Code Bad: Widget build(BuildContext ctx) { return Column(children: [ Container(child: Row(children: [ ... long nested tree ... ])) ]); }

Description: ListView.builder lazily builds items only when visible; ListView with children list eagerly instantiates all items regardless of viewport.
Do: Use ListView.builder with itemCount and itemBuilder for all lists with 3+ items
Don't: Use ListView(children: items.map(...).toList()) for long lists
Code Good: ListView.builder(itemCount: items.length, itemBuilder: (ctx, i) => ItemTile(item: items[i]))

Guideline: Use SliverList for complex scroll layouts
Description: SliverList inside CustomScrollView enables mixing lists with headers
Do: grids
Don't: and slivers without nesting scroll views.
Code Good: Use CustomScrollView + SliverList + SliverAppBar for screens with collapsing headers
Code Bad: Nest ListView inside SingleChildScrollView; use NestedScrollView without understanding its constraints

Description: Large lists must use virtualized rendering. Consider using flutter_virtual_listview for very large datasets to prevent memory issues.
Do: Use ListView.builder or SliverList; consider page-based loading for 500+ items
Don't: Render all items at once in a Column; use ListView with children for large datasets

Description: Wrap independently animating widgets in RepaintBoundary to isolate repaints and prevent unnecessary repaints of surrounding widgets.
Do: Wrap AnimatedWidget, Lottie, and custom animation subtrees in RepaintBoundary
Don't: Skip RepaintBoundary on all animated widgets; animate the entire screen

Description: MediaQuery.of(context) rebuilds on every query call inside build. Cache the result in a local variable at the top of build().
Do: Cache in variable at the top of build: final mq = MediaQuery.of(context);
Don't: Call MediaQuery.of(context).size.width multiple times inside loops or deep in widget trees
Code Good: final size = MediaQuery.of(context).size; final w = size.width;

Description: Raw Image.network has no caching; cached_network_image caches to disk and memory with placeholder and error support.
Do: Use CachedNetworkImage for all remote images with placeholder and errorWidget
Don't: Use Image.network directly in production apps
Code Good: CachedNetworkImage(imageUrl: url, placeholder: (ctx, url) => Shimmer(...), errorWidget: (ctx, url, e) => Icon(Icons.error))

Description: Animating width/height/padding triggers layout recalculation on every frame. Use transform and opacity instead — these are compositing operations.
Do: Animate transform and opacity only; use AnimatedPositioned only when truly needed
Don't: Animate width/height directly with AnimatedContainer for frequent animations

Description: All visual tokens (colors
Do: text styles
Don't: shapes
Code Good: elevations) must be defined in ThemeData and accessed via Theme.of(context). Never hardcode in widgets.
Code Bad: Define colorScheme, textTheme, cardTheme, inputDecorationTheme at app level; access via Theme.of(context)
Severity: Hardcode Color(0xFF1A1A2E) or TextStyle(fontSize: 14) directly in widgets

Description: Define independent ThemeData for light and dark. Never derive dark from light at runtime by inverting colors. Dark mode needs independent design decisions.
Do: MaterialApp(theme: lightTheme, darkTheme: darkTheme, themeMode: ThemeMode.system)
Don't: Use Theme.of(context).brightness to invert light theme colors at runtime
Code Good: ThemeData(brightness: Brightness.dark, colorScheme: ColorScheme.fromSeed(seedColor: brandColor, brightness: Brightness.dark))

Description: Inconsistent border radius and spacing values are the #1 cause of visually unprofessional apps. Define as constants and use everywhere.
Do: Define kRadiusSm=8.0 kRadiusMd=12.0 kRadiusLg=20.0 and 4/8dp spacing constants
Don't: Use arbitrary BorderRadius.circular(6)/circular(9)/circular(11) per widget

Description: Google Fonts provides 1400+ fonts without asset bundling. Use distinctive pairings — never ship with default Roboto for the entire app.
Do: Use GoogleFonts for display/heading fonts; pair with a refined body font via GoogleFonts.xyzTextTheme()
Don't: Default to Roboto system font for all text; use the same font for headings and body

Description: Map fonts to TextTheme roles (displayLarge/titleMedium/bodyMedium/labelSmall) in ThemeData so all Text widgets automatically use the design system.
Do: Extend theme styles with copyWith: Theme.of(context).textTheme.bodyMedium!.copyWith(color: ...)
Don't: Create inline TextStyle instances in widgets; ignore ThemeData.textTheme

Description: Flutter respects MediaQuery.textScaleFactor by default. Never use fixed-height containers around text or it will clip when user increases font size.
Do: Use Flexible/Expanded around text containers; avoid SizedBox with fixed height around Text
Don't: Wrap Text in SizedBox(height: 20) that cannot grow; disable textScaleFactor

Description: Icon-only buttons are invisible to screen readers without explicit semantic labels. Every IconButton must have tooltip or semantics label.
Do: Add tooltip: 'Close' to IconButton; or wrap in Semantics(label: 'Close', button: true)
Don't: Use bare Icon inside GestureDetector without any semantics
Code Good: IconButton(icon: Icon(Icons.close), tooltip: 'Close dialog', onPressed: onClose)

Description: Decorative images and dividers add noise to screen reader traversal. Mark them explicitly as non-semantic.
Do: Use Image(excludeFromSemantics: true) for decorative images; wrap in ExcludeSemantics
Don't: Include decorative images with empty or missing semanticsLabel

Description: Users who enable Reduce Motion on iOS or Remove Animations on Android expect animations to be reduced or eliminated. Always check MediaQuery.disableAnimations.
Do: Check MediaQuery.of(context).disableAnimations before running animations; provide instant alternatives
Don't: Ignore disableAnimations; always play animations regardless of accessibility settings

Description: Small touch targets cause mis-taps and fail accessibility guidelines. iOS minimum is 44×44pt; Material minimum is 48×48dp.
Do: Use kMinInteractiveDimension constant; expand hit area with Padding inside GestureDetector using HitTestBehavior.opaque
Don't: Use 20×20dp icon without expanded hit area
Code Good: GestureDetector(behavior: HitTestBehavior.opaque, child: Padding(padding: EdgeInsets.all(12), child: Icon(Icons.info, size: 20)))

Description: Every tappable element needs visual feedback within 100ms of press. Material uses InkWell ripple; Cupertino uses opacity highlight.
Do: Wrap tappable widgets in InkWell (Material) or use CupertinoButton; add AnimatedScale for custom components
Don't: Use GestureDetector alone on custom tappable components with no visual state change
Code Good: InkWell(onTap: onTap, borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(12), child: Padding(...))

Description: Haptic feedback reinforces important interactions and improves perceived quality. Use HapticFeedback built-in — no package needed.
Do: Call HapticFeedback.mediumImpact() on confirmations, HapticFeedback.lightImpact() on selections
Don't: Omit haptics entirely; use heavy impact for minor interactions
Code Good: HapticFeedback.mediumImpact(); // in onTap for primary actions

Description: SafeArea insets content away from notch
Do: Dynamic Island
Don't: status bar
Code Good: and gesture bar. Missing SafeArea causes content to appear under system chrome.
Code Bad: Wrap Scaffold body or its children in SafeArea; use SafeArea for fixed bottom bars outside Scaffold
Severity: Omit SafeArea; use hardcoded top padding like EdgeInsets.only(top: 44)

Description: LayoutBuilder provides constraints for the current widget's available space — more accurate than MediaQuery.size for components inside complex layouts.
Do: Use LayoutBuilder for responsive component behavior; MediaQuery for screen-level decisions
Don't: Use MediaQuery.of(context).size inside deep widget trees for component-level responsiveness
Code Good: LayoutBuilder(builder: (ctx, constraints) { if (constraints.maxWidth > 600) return WideLayout(); return NarrowLayout(); })

Description: Use Scaffold.bottomNavigationBar to place bottom bars — Scaffold automatically insets scroll content. Manual padding causes double-inset bugs.
Do: Pass BottomNavigationBar/NavigationBar to Scaffold.bottomNavigationBar
Don't: Add manual Padding(bottom: kBottomNavigationBarHeight) inside scroll views when using Scaffold.bottomNavigationBar

Description: Navigator 1.0 push/pop cannot deep link or restore state from system navigation. go_router provides declarative routing with deep link support.
Do: Define GoRouter with GoRoute paths; use context.go() and context.push()
Don't: Use only Navigator.push/pop for all navigation; no named routes or deep linking

Description: More than 5 items in a bottom nav creates cognitive overload and visual cramping. Use Drawer for secondary navigation.
Do: Use NavigationBar (M3) or BottomNavigationBar with 3-5 top-level items only
Don't: Add 6+ items to bottom nav; omit labels from bottom nav items

Description: Android 13+ has gesture-based predictive back. Implement PopScope to confirm navigation on screens with unsaved changes.
Do: Wrap screen in PopScope(canPop: false, onPopInvokedWithResult: ...) to show confirm dialog before leaving
Don't: Ignore back gesture handling; let Navigator pop automatically on all screens
Code Good: PopScope(canPop: false, onPopInvokedWithResult: (didPop, _) { if (!didPop) _showDiscardDialog(context); }, child: screen)

Description: CupertinoPageRoute enables iOS native swipe-back. MaterialPageRoute blocks it on iOS. go_router uses CupertinoPage for iOS targets.
Do: Use CupertinoPage or CupertinoPageRoute on iOS; go_router handles this automatically
Don't: Use MaterialPageRoute for all platforms including iOS without Cupertino variant
Code Bad: Navigator.push(ctx, MaterialPageRoute(builder: (_) => DetailScreen())) // blocks iOS swipe-back

Description: flutter_animate provides a clean declarative API for common animations (fade
Do: slide
Don't: scale
Code Good: shimmer) with stagger support and reduced boilerplate.
Code Bad: Use .animate().fadeIn().slideY() chain; use delay: for stagger effects
Severity: Write AnimationController + Tween + AnimatedBuilder boilerplate for every simple fade/slide
column10: AnimationController(vsync: this, duration: 300ms)..forward(); FadeTransition(opacity: _anim, child: Text('Hello'))

Description: Items appearing simultaneously feel abrupt. Stagger by 30-50ms per item for a polished list reveal.
Do: Use delay: (index * 50).ms in .animate() chain for each list item
Don't: Animate all list items simultaneously with the same duration and no delay

Description: go_router's CustomTransitionPage enables app-wide consistent page transitions. Default MaterialPage transition feels generic on iOS.
Do: Define custom pageBuilder with CustomTransitionPage for key routes; use slide for forward, fade for modal
Don't: Use default page transitions for all routes; mix different transition types randomly
Code Good: CustomTransitionPage(child: Screen(), transitionsBuilder: (ctx, anim, _, child) => SlideTransition(..., child: child))

Description: Placeholder-only inputs fail when the user starts typing (label disappears) and fail accessibility guidelines. Always use labelText.
Do: Use InputDecoration with labelText (floating label) and optional helperText; add errorText for validation
Don't: Use hintText as the only label; omit labelText entirely

Description: Wrong keyboard type forces users to switch keyboard layout manually. Always set keyboardType and textInputAction.
Do: Set keyboardType: TextInputType.emailAddress for email, TextInputType.phone for phone, TextInputType.number for numbers
Don't: Use TextInputType.text for all inputs including email, phone, and URL

Description: Platform autofill (iOS Keychain
Do: Android Autofill Framework) works only when autofillHints is set. Dramatically improves onboarding conversion.
Don't: Set autofillHints: const [AutofillHints.email] for email, AutofillHints.password for password, etc.
Code Good: Omit autofillHints on login, signup, and checkout forms

Description: Irreversible actions (delete
Do: logout
Don't: clear data) must be confirmed with a dialog. Use CupertinoAlertDialog on iOS
Code Bad: Show showDialog with confirm/cancel options before any destructive operation
Severity: Execute delete/logout immediately on button tap with no confirmation
Docs URL: showDialog(context: ctx, builder: (_) => AlertDialog(title: Text('Delete item?'), actions: [TextButton(child: Text('Cancel'), ...), TextButton(child: Text('Delete'), ...)]))

Description: Flutter renders Material widgets on all platforms by default. For iOS feel use Cupertino equivalents. go_router and platform checks enable adaptive UI.
Do: Check Theme.of(context).platform or use adaptive constructors (Switch.adaptive, CircularProgressIndicator.adaptive)
Don't: Use Material widgets only with no platform consideration; ignore iOS HIG on iOS builds
Code Bad: Switch(value: val, onChanged: onChanged) // always Material

Description: AlertDialog follows Material Design. CupertinoAlertDialog follows iOS HIG. Platform-correct dialogs improve perceived quality on each OS.
Do: Use showAdaptiveDialog or showCupertinoDialog on iOS, showDialog on Android
Don't: Show AlertDialog (Material) on iOS devices
Code Bad: showDialog(context: ctx, builder: (_) => AlertDialog(...)) // on all platforms

Description: Images without declared dimensions cause layout shift (CLS equivalent in Flutter). Always reserve space with AspectRatio or width/height.
Do: Set explicit width/height or wrap in AspectRatio widget for all images
Don't: Let Image widget determine its own size from network response; causes layout jump

Description: Images shown immediately on page load should be precached to prevent a visible loading flash on first render.
Do: Call precacheImage(NetworkImage(url), context) in initState for above-the-fold images
Don't: Load hero images only when widget builds; accept the flash on first load
